agentMessage.excerpt:
Review checklist → pass
✓  Build completed — the implementor finished in 22 round(s)
✓  Change is visible — 3 file(s) changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, README.md, docs/API.md
✓  Changed the deliverable's target files — expected ≥1 of: README.md, docs/API.md, docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md
✓  Verification passed — the project verify command was GREEN
✓  Work committed — committed locally on the build branch
0.90  Implements the approved plan — All three acceptance criteria are met: PROVIDER_IDS.md exists with stable-contract language ('provider IDs are a stable contract — consumers can depend on them'), a deprecation policy with ≥4-week window where deprecated IDs remain functional, and a changelog expectation. Linked from README and API.md. Minor gap: the plan specifically calls for 'both old and new IDs served concurrently' for renames — the doc covers deprecation/retirement thoroughly but does not explicitly illustrate the rename scenario with dual-service. The mechanisms (additions announced, deprecated IDs stay functional) effectively cover it.
1.00  Minimal and scoped — Only 3 documentation files changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (new), README.md (one bullet link added), docs/API.md (See also section added). No source code touched, no unrelated churn — exactly the scope prescribed by the plan.
1.00  No test-gaming / faking — The new doc is 105 lines of substantive, real content: canonical provider table, stability contract, deprecation lifecycle, changelog expectations, and a non-governed-IDs scoping section. No placeholder text, no tests weakened or deleted — the verify command passing is legitimate.
1.00  Safe to advance — Documentation is accurate, well-scoped, and introduces no correctness or security risk. It clearly distinguishes governed provider IDs from non-governed upstream voice IDs and flags the JSON artifact as the source of truth, which is a responsible disclaimer.
0.95  Judge confidence — at or above the confidence bar
